data.title: French industry minister committed to 2035 fossil-fuel car regulations, but open to softening them
data.description:
French industry minister Marc Ferracci said on Thursday he was open to discussions on relaxing regulations that will bar the sale of fossil-fuel cars by 2035.

Ferracci told lawmakers at a parliamentary hearing that he was "ready" to think about relaxing the ban, though he added that the target should remain and that he was committed to the goal of decarbonisation.

It was not immediately clear what steps France would take. The EU has set a 2035 target that all new cars and vans sold in the European Union no longer emit carbon dioxide.

Europe's auto industry has faced slowing demand growth for electric vehicles and lower-cost Chinese rivals and have argued more government subsidies and more widespread charging infrastructure is needed to increase demand for EVs.

data.title: Corn Rebounds From 6-Month Lows
data.description:
Corn futures rose to around $4.40 per bushel rebounding from a six-month low of $4.335 seen June 9th, as US supply concerns and firm demand dynamics outweigh ample global harvest prospects.

The USDA’s June supply-and-demand update trimmed 2024/25 ending stocks by 1.3 million tonnes to 44.5 million, underscoring tighter domestic balance sheets, while weekly export inspections have consistently exceeded forecasts, signaling strong overseas appetite.

Meanwhile, continued dryness across the US Midwest, where just 66% of the crop was rated good to excellent in early June versus 72% last year, has raised fresh yield risks as the critical pollination window approaches.

Ethanol crack spreads, supported by healthy refinery margins, are also propping up corn’s biofuel demand.

Offsetting these drivers, Brazil’s “safrinha” was raised by Conab to a record 101 Mt and Black Sea forecasts for 2025 (Romania and Ukraine) have jumped over 70% year-on-year, capping any runaway rally.

data.title: BARRETT BUSINESS SERVICES Director Increases Stake
data.description:
Reporter NameFinn Mark Steven
RelationshipDirector
TypePurchase
Amount$84,392
SEC FilingForm 4

Mark Steven Finn, a Director at BARRETT BUSINESS SERVICES, purchased a total of 2,000 shares of Common Stock on June 10 and June 11, 2025, for a total purchase amount of $84,392. The price per share for the transactions was $42.4636 and $41.9289, respectively. Following these transactions, Finn directly owns 13,850 shares of BARRETT BUSINESS SERVICES.

SEC Filing: BARRETT BUSINESS SERVICES INC [ BBSI ] - Form 4 - Jun. 12, 2025

data.title: Sector Update: Health Care Stocks Advance in Afternoon Trading
data.description:
Health care stocks were higher Thursday afternoon, with the NYSE Health Care Index rising 0.8% and the Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLV) up 0.5%.

The iShares Biotechnology ETF (IBB) advanced 0.8%.

In corporate news, BioNTech (BNTX) agreed to buy CureVac in an all-stock deal with an equity value of about $1.25 billion to strengthen its oncology portfolio. CureVac shares jumped 34%, and BioNTech added 0.4%.

Cardinal Health lifted its fiscal 2025 earnings guidance and provided a preliminary bottom-line outlook above market estimates at the midpoint for the following year. Its shares popped 4.4%.

Moderna is in talks with pharmaceutical and financial companies on funding late-stage vaccine trials, part of its broader strategy to expand its portfolio while cutting costs, Chief Executive Officer Stephane Bancel said Wednesday at a conference. Moderna shares were down 0.4%.
